title: Shape, Border, Fit, Zoom & Focus
description: Five brand-new browser-native primitives: CSS shape() morphing, native gradient borders, an auto-fit text-fit headline, animatable zoom, and a zero-JS focusgroup toolbar.

drops.title: Shape() Morph Deck
drops.description: Three shapes — a wave banner, a spiked badge, a speech bubble — animated purely with the CSS shape() function.
drops.concept: The new clip-path: shape() function draws curved, responsive paths directly in CSS using SVG-like from/curve/line/close commands. Pairing it with @property-registered custom properties makes the path itself keyframe-animatable, so a wave crest, a pulsing star spike and a speech-bubble tail all morph smoothly with zero SVG or JS tweening.
drops.source: Chrome for Developers — "Use shape() for responsive clipping"

interaction.mouse: Watch the three shapes morph continuously; adjust the speed slider to change the pace
interaction.click: Pause all toggles the animations on/off
interaction.touch: Fully passive on touch — the debug panel is hidden on mobile

drops.title: Native Gradient Border Bento
drops.description: A bento grid where every card's spinning gradient border is painted natively with background-clip: border-area.
drops.concept: background-clip now accepts border-area, letting a background layer paint directly onto the border ring instead of being sliced into nine segments like border-image. Two comma-separated background-image layers — one clipped to padding-box for the fill, one clipped to border-area for a rotating conic gradient — build a live gradient-bordered bento grid with no pseudo-elements or wrapper divs.
drops.source: WebKit Blog — "Make creative borders with background-clip: border-area"

interaction.mouse: Watch the conic gradient borders spin; drag the width/speed sliders to retune them
interaction.click: Pause stops the spin so you can inspect the border ring
interaction.touch: Grid reflows to two columns on small screens

drops.title: Auto-Fit Headline
drops.description: Editable headline, quote and caption blocks that grow or shrink live to fill their container edge-to-edge.
drops.concept: The proposed CSS text-fit property scales font-size so a line fills its container width exactly, in grow/shrink and consistent/per-line modes — eliminating the canvas-measurement JS hacks publishers currently use for responsive headlines. This drop implements both a native text-fit path (behind a CSS.supports check) and a canvas-measurement JS emulation, so the effect fires identically whichever the browser supports.

interaction.mouse: Click into any block and type — the text re-fits to the container width on every keystroke
interaction.click: Mode buttons switch between grow and shrink fitting
interaction.touch: Tap to focus and use the on-screen keyboard, same live re-fit

drops.title: Zoom-Pop Interface
drops.description: A dock, a lens toggle and press-pop cards built on the newly-animatable CSS zoom property.
drops.concept: Chrome 150 shipped animatable zoom — the CSS zoom property now smoothly interpolates as a transition target. Unlike transform: scale(), zoom is a real layout property, so neighboring elements genuinely reflow as an element zooms, and content zoomed inside a clipped frame enlarges at true pixel scale. Three instances show the difference: a dock row where hovering one icon visibly shifts its neighbors, a lens toggle that zooms content within a fixed frame, and press-pop cards that pulse zoom on click.
drops.source: Chromium blink-dev — "Intent to Ship: Animatable zoom"

interaction.mouse: Hover a dock icon to feel siblings shift; click the lens button or any card
interaction.click: Toggle lens zooms the framed content; clicking a card triggers a zoom pulse
interaction.touch: Tap targets replace hover on touch devices

drops.title: Zero-JS Focusgroup Toolbar
drops.description: A toolbar, a menu and a radiogroup grid with native roving-tabindex arrow-key navigation via the focusgroup attribute.
drops.concept: The focusgroup HTML attribute implements the 'roving tabindex' pattern natively — tab into a group once, then arrow keys move focus between items while the group stays a single tab stop, with the browser remembering the last-focused item. This drop declares focusgroup on three real composite widgets (toolbar, menu, radiogroup grid) and ships a matching roving-tabindex JS implementation so the keyboard behavior works identically whether or not the browser has shipped native focusgroup support yet.

interaction.mouse: Click any item to activate it and set it as the tab stop
interaction.click: Arrow keys move focus within each group; Enter/Space activates the focused item
interaction.touch: Tap targets are large enough for touch activation
